perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information does not include, or shall cease to include, information or material that the Recipient can demonstrate by then- contemporaneous written records: (i) is in the Recipient’s possession at the time of disclosure as shown by the Recipient’s files and records immediately prior to the time of disclosure; (ii) before or after it has been disclosed to Recipient, becomes part of public knowledge or literature, not as a result of any action or inaction of the Recipient; (iii) is approved for release by written authorization of the Disclosing Party; (iv) is disclosed to the R ecipient by a third party not in violation of any obligation of confidentiality; or (v) is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information does not include information or material that the Recipient can demonstrate. However, all information exchanged will be considered Confidential Information, superseding any previous knowledge or public availability, unless otherwise specified in writing by the Disclosing Party. The Disclosing Party has sole discretion in determining what constitutes Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that all information exchanged will be considered confidential, regardless of prior knowledge or public availability, directly contradicting the original exclusions listed in the original text. This introduces uncertainty because if the Recipient can demonstrate prior knowledge per the original exclusion, it's still Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exclusions. Confidential Information does not include, or shall cease to include, information or material that the Recipient can demonstrate by then- contemporaneous written records: (i) is in the Recipient’s possession at the time of disclosure as shown by the Recipient’s files and records immediately prior to the time of disclosure; (ii) before or after it has been disclosed to Recipient, becomes part of public knowledge or literature, not as a result of any action or inaction of the Recipient; (iii) is approved for release by written authorization of the Disclosing Party; (iv) is disclosed to the R ecipient by a third party not in violation of any obligation of confidentiality; or (v) is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a contradiction because it states that all information exchanged will be considered confidential, regardless of prior knowledge or public availability. This directly contradicts the exclusions listed in the original text and the contradicted text, which states that information already in the Recipient's possession or public knowledge is not considered confidential. This creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding what information is considered confidential.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient (i) agrees not to use the Confidential Information for its own use or for any purposes except the Authorized Purpose expressly set forth above; (ii) shall not use the Confidential Information for purposes of unfair or improper competition; and (iii) agrees not to copy, alter, modify, disassemble, reverse engineer or decompile any of the materials unless permitted in writing by the Disclosing Party (any copies that are authorized to be made will be identified as belonging to th e Disclosing Party and marked as “confidential”, “proprietary” or with similar legend). Nothing in this Agreement shall limit or restrict the rights of the Disclosing Party to assert infringement or other intellectual property claims against the Recipien t or to impose on either party any obligation to disclose any Confident ial Information, to purchase or sell any products, or to otherwise enter in to any type of business relationship.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient (i) agrees not to use the Confidential Information for its own use or for any purposes except the Authorized Purpose expressly set forth above; (ii) shall not use the Confidential Information for purposes of unfair or improper competition; and (iii) agrees not to copy, alter, modify, disassemble, reverse engineer or decompile any of the materials unless permitted in writing by the Disclosing Party (any copies that are authorized to be made will be identified as belonging to th e Disclosing Party and marked as “confidential”, “proprietary” or with similar legend). The Recipient may use the Confidential Information to file for intellectual property protection in its own name. Nothing in this Agreement shall limit or restrict the rights of the Disclosing Party to assert infringement or other intellectual property claims against the Recipien t or to impose on either party any obligation to disclose any Confident ial Information, to purchase or sell any products, or to otherwise enter in to any type of business relationship.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a conflict regarding the use of Confidential Information. The original text restricts the Recipient's use to the Authorized Purpose and prohibits modification. The added sentence allows the Recipient to use the same Confidential Information for intellectual property filings in their name. This creates uncertainty about ownership and usage rights, directly contradicting the purpose of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No License. The Disclosing Party shall retain the sole ownership and right to possess its Confidential Information, and any derivatives thereof. No license is granted to the Recipient under any patents, copyrights, mask w ork rights or other proprietary rights by the disclosure of any information hereunder, nor is any warranty made as to such information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence stating, "The Recipient may use the Confidential Information to file for intellectual property protection in its own name." This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states, "The Disclosing Party shall retain the sole ownership and right to possess its Confidential Information, and any derivatives thereof. No license is granted to the Recipient..." The changed text now allows the Recipient to act as the owner in obtaining IP protections for confidential information, which the contradicted text reserves exclusively for the Disclosing Party. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists based on the Structural Flaws definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third parties or to any of its employees, dire ctors, officers, agents, contractors, consultants, advisors, or other representatives (collectively “ Representatives ”), except those Representatives who have a need to know the Confidential Information for accomplishing the Authorized Purpose described her ein. In such instance(s), the Representatives (i) shall be made aware that the Confidential Information is confidential; and (ii) that such Representatives’ disclosure of the Confidential Information is restricted as set forth herein. Notwithsta nding the foregoing, the Recipient may disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to the extent required by a valid order of a court of competent jurisdiction or other governmental body, or by applicable law; provided, however, that the Recipient will use all reasonable efforts to promptly notify the Disclosing Party of the obligation to make such disclosure in advance of the disclosure, unless otherwise prohibited by law or court order, so that the Disclosing Party will have a reasonable opportunity to object to such disclosure. The Recipient and its Representatives shall cooperate in all reasonable respects with the Disclosing Party in seeking to prevent or limit disclosure; and, in the event that a protective order or other remedy is not obtained, t he Recipient will limit disclosure to the Confidential Information actually required to be disclosed, provided the Recipient exercises its reasonable efforts to obtain reasonable assurances that confidential treatment will be accorded to the Disclosing Par ty’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third parties or to any of its employees, dire ctors, officers, agents, contractors, consultants, advisors, or other representatives (collectively “ Representatives ”), except those Representatives who have a need to know the Confidential Information for accomplishing the Authorized Purpose described her ein. In such instance(s), the Representatives (i) shall be made aware that the Confidential Information is confidential; and (ii) that such Representatives’ disclosure of the Confidential Information is restricted as set forth herein. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient may disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to anyone for marketing purposes with no restrictions. Notwithsta nding the foregoing, the Recipient may disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to the extent required by a valid order of a court of competent jurisdiction or other governmental body, or by applicable law; provided, however, that the Recipient will use all reasonable efforts to promptly notify the Disclosing Party of the obligation to make such disclosure in advance of the disclosure, unless otherwise prohibited by law or court order, so that the Disclosing Party will have a reasonable opportunity to object to such disclosure. The Recipient and its Representatives shall cooperate in all reasonable respects with the Disclosing Party in seeking to prevent or limit disclosure; and, in the event that a protective order or other remedy is not obtained, t he Recipient will limit disclosure to the Confidential Information actually required to be disclosed, provided the Recipient exercises its reasonable efforts to obtain reasonable assurances that confidential treatment will be accorded to the Disclosing Par ty’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the sentence 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient may disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to anyone for marketing purposes with no restrictions,' this directly contradicts the initial statement that disclosure is restricted to Representatives with a 'need to know' for the Authorized Purpose. This introduces significant uncertainty about the scope of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non -Disclosure. The Recipient agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third parties or to any of its employees, dire ctors, officers, agents, contractors, consultants, advisors, or other representatives (collectively “ Representatives ”), except those Representatives who have a need to know the Confidential Information for accomplishing the Authorized Purpose described her ein. In such instance(s), the Representatives (i) shall be made aware that the Confidential Information is confidential; and (ii) that such Representatives’ disclosure of the Confidential Information is restricted as set forth herein. Notwithsta nding the foregoing, the Recipient may disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to anyone for marketing purposes with no restrictions. Notwithsta nding the foregoing, the Recipient may disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to the extent required by a valid order of a court of competent jurisdiction or other governmental body, or by applicable law; provided, however, that the Recipient will use all reasonable efforts to promptly notify the Disclosing Party of the obligation to make such disclosure in advance of the disclosure, unless otherwise prohibited by law or court order, so that the Disclosing Party will have a reasonable opportunity to object to such disclosure. The Recipient and its Representatives shall cooperate in all reasonable respects with the Disclosing Party in seeking to prevent or limit disclosure; and, in the event that a protective order or other remedy is not obtained, t he Recipient will limit disclosure to the Confidential Information actually required to be disclosed, provided the Recipient exercises its reasonable efforts to obtain reasonable assurances that confidential treatment will be accorded to the Disclosing Par ty’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause allowing disclosure for marketing purposes with no restrictions, directly contradicting the initial agreement in both the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text, which limits disclosure to 'Representatives' with a 'need to know' for the 'Authorized Purpose'. The addition creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict, making the confidentiality terms ambiguous and unenforceable. The perturbation explanation confirms this direct contradiction, falling under the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' as the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Any materials or documents of the Disclosing Party that are furnished to the Recipient, and all copies thereof, at the earlier of the Disclosing Party’s request for return of the materials, or the termination of the business relationship between the Disclosing Party and the Recipient, at the Disclosing Party’s option, will either be (subject to the Disclosing P arty’s reasonable instructions): (i) promptl y returned to the Disclosing Party and/or (ii) destroyed by the Recipient, including materials stored on the Recipient’s computing environment, or electronic storage media, to the extent technically feasible (with the Recipient providing written certification of such destruction).
perturbation.changed_text: Any materials or documents of the Disclosing Party that are furnished to the Recipient, and all copies thereof, at the earlier of the Disclosing Party’s request for return of the materials, or the termination of the business relationship between the Disclosing Party and the Recipient, the Recipient will retain a copy of all Confidential Information indefinitely. The Recipient also retains the right to use previously returned information, for internal reference. at the Disclosing Party’s option, will either be (subject to the Disclosing P arty’s reasonable instructions): (i) promptl y returned to the Disclosing Party and/or (ii) destroyed by the Recipient, including materials stored on the Recipient’s computing environment, or electronic storage media, to the extent technically feasible (with the Recipient providing written certification of such destruction).
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the Recipient will retain a copy of all Confidential Information indefinitely and can use it, even though the original text states that the materials must be returned or destroyed at the request of the Disclosing Party or on termination. The text now is contradictory regarding the ultimate disposition of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Materials. Any materials or documents of the Disclosing Party that are furnished to the Recipient, and all copies thereof, at the earlier of the Disclosing Party’s request for return of the materials, or the termination of the business relationship between the Disclosing Party and the Recipient, the Recipient will retain a copy of all Confidential Information indefinitely. The Recipient also retains the right to use previously returned information, for internal reference. at the Disclosing Party’s option, will either be (subject to the Disclosing P arty’s reasonable instructions): (i) promptl y returned to the Disclosing Party and/or (ii) destroyed by the Recipient, including materials stored on the Recipient’s computing environment, or electronic storage media, to the extent technically feasible (with the Recipient providing written certification of such destruction).
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the statement 'the Recipient will retain a copy of all Confidential Information indefinitely. The Recipient also retains the right to use previously returned information, for internal reference.' This directly contradicts the requirement in the same paragraph and contradicted text that upon request or termination, the Recipient must either return or destroy the confidential information. The changed text therefore creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient understands and agrees that the Disclosing Party is providing the Confidential Informat ion to the Recipient in reliance upon this Agreement, and the Recipient will be fully responsible to the Disclosing Party for any damage s or harm caused to the Disclosing Party by a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or any of its Representatives. T he Recipient acknowledges and agrees that a breach of any of its promises or agreements contained herein will result in irreparable injury to the Disclosing Party for which there will be no adequate remedy at law, and the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including specific performance and injunctive relief, in the event of any breach or threatened breach or intended breach of t his Agreement by the Recipient. Such remedies, however, shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for any breach of the Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient understands and agrees that the Disclosing Party is providing the Confidential Informat ion to the Recipient in reliance upon this Agreement, and the Recipient will only be responsible to the Disclosing Party for any direct damages, but the maximum amount is not greater than 1000 USD, caused to the Disclosing Party by a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or any of its Representatives. T he Recipient acknowledges and agrees that a breach of any of its promises or agreements contained herein will result in irreparable injury to the Disclosing Party for which there will be no adequate remedy at law, and the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including specific performance and injunctive relief, in the event of any breach or threatened breach or intended breach of t his Agreement by the Recipient. Such remedies, however, shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for any breach of the Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: This change places a limitation on the responsibility of the recipient for damages caused by a breach of the agreement, limiting it to direct damages not greater than 1000 USD. This contradicts the later statement that a breach will result in irreparable injury to the disclosing party for which there will be no adequate remedy at law, where the change contradicts where the responsibility of the breach will not be as what it was meant to be.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies. The Recipient understands and agrees that the Disclosing Party is providing the Confidential Informat ion to the Recipient in reliance upon this Agreement, and the Recipient will only be responsible to the Disclosing Party for any direct damages, but the maximum amount is not greater than 1000 USD, caused to the Disclosing Party by a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or any of its Representatives. T he Recipient acknowledges and agrees that a breach of any of its promises or agreements contained herein will result in irreparable injury to the Disclosing Party for which there will be no adequate remedy at law, and the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including specific performance and injunctive relief, in the event of any breach or threatened breach or intended breach of t his Agreement by the Recipient. Such remedies, however, shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for any breach of the Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the recipient's liability to direct damages not exceeding $1000, which directly contradicts the contradicted text's assertion that a breach will result in irreparable injury with no adequate remedy at law, implying potentially much higher damages and equitable remedies. The original text stated that the Recipient will be fully responsible to the Disclosing Party for any damages or harm. The change introduces a limitation that directly clashes with the concept of 'irreparable injury' and the availability of 'equitable relief' as stated in the original text and repeated in the contradicted text. This is a clear in-text contradiction under Structural Flaws.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall govern all communications among the parties that are made from the date Confidential Inf ormation was first provided to or obtained by the Recipient (even if prior to the Effective Date) through the date on which a party receives from the other party written notice that such party is terminating this Agreement. The Recipient’s obligations und er this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information it has received or obtained prior to such termination shall continue until as long as the Confidential Information remains a trade secret or for a period of five (5) years after the dat e of disclos ure, whichever is later.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall govern all communications among the parties that are made from the date Confidential Inf ormation was first provided to or obtained by the Recipient (even if prior to the Effective Date) through the date on which a party receives from the other party written notice that such party is terminating this Agreement. The Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information it has received or obtained prior to such termination shall cease immediately upon termination regardless if the information still remains a trade secret. shall continue until as long as the Confidential Information remains a trade secret or for a period of five (5) years after the dat e of disclos ure, whichever is later.
perturbation.explanation: The original text indicates that confidentiality obligations continue after termination, either for the duration the information is a trade secret or for five years. By Inserting that 'The Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information it has received or obtained prior to such termination shall cease immediately upon termination regardless if the information still remains a trade secret.' directly conflicts with the the initial sentence in the orignal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term; Termination. This Agreement shall govern all communications among the parties that are made from the date Confidential Inf ormation was first provided to or obtained by the Recipient (even if prior to the Effective Date) through the date on which a party receives from the other party written notice that such party is terminating this Agreement. The Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information it has received or obtained prior to such termination shall cease immediately upon termination regardless if the information still remains a trade secret. shall continue until as long as the Confidential Information remains a trade secret or for a period of five (5) years after the dat e of disclos ure, whichever is later.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text stated that confidentiality obligations continue after termination under certain conditions. The changed text explicitly states that the Recipient’s obligations cease immediately upon termination, regardless of whether the information remains a trade secret. This directly contradicts the portion of the original text and contradicted text that says that these obligations 'shall continue until as long as the Confidential Information remains a trade secret or for a period of five (5) years after the date of disclosure, whichever is later.'
